summary: Verified Cloudflare 520 hardening of /api/files/search. Built /app/backend/tests/test_iter38_files_search.py (16 tests, 16 passed): franchisee + admin login both work via /api/auth/login; the exact production repro 'Armed Forces Day' as franchisee now returns HTTP 200 with the {items, files, folders, count} envelope; single-word + multi-word queries also return 200; short query (q='a') correctly returns 422 (Pydantic), not 5xx; admin global + folder-scoped search regression both pass; 8 adversarial inputs (unicode, regex-meta chars, emoji, dotted, path-ish, punctuation-only, padded spaces) all return <500 with valid envelopes. The original CF-520 root cause path is now closed: no unhandled exception can bubble up.

ui_bugs.issue: Franchisee tester credentials (franchisee.tester@creativemojo.co.uk / FranchiseeTest2026!) are accepted by /api/auth/login (token issued, backend tests pass) but rejected by the portal login pre-check with the message 'We couldn't find a portal account for that email. Please check the address or contact your administrator.' This blocks end-to-end UI verification of /portal/files via the portal flow but does NOT affect the backend /api/files/search fix — that is fully verified via the API tests.

action_items: Investigate why the portal login lookup ('We couldn't find a portal account for that email') rejects franchisee.tester@creativemojo.co.uk while /api/auth/login accepts the same credentials. The franchisee_id (febd57cf-600d-4b44-bebc-6a9177984832) is present in test_credentials.md — the portal user/contact link probably isn't seeded on preview. This is a SEPARATE issue from the Cloudflare 520 fix and does not regress it.
Cloudflare 520 fix itself needs NO further action — all 16 API tests pass including the exact prod repro query.
critical_code_review_comments: files_routes.py files_search outer try/except correctly re-raises HTTPException so FastAPI's 422 validation path is preserved (good).
walk_cap=20_000 prevents unbounded folder walks behind Cloudflare's 100s proxy limit (good).
Orphan-franchisee (HTTPException from _franchisee_scope_filter) → degraded 200 with structured 'error' field is a good UX choice; matches the frontend's results.error display.
Folder-walk filter correctly composes scope_clause via $and, so franchisees can no longer see folder names they can't browse — regression-safe.

success_rate.frontend: N/A — UI not exercised due to portal-login seed gap; backend behaviour verified directly via API

test_credentials: Admin admin@creativemojo.co.uk / CreativeMojo2026! and franchisee franchisee.tester@creativemojo.co.uk / FranchiseeTest2026! both authenticate against /api/auth/login successfully. Portal /portal/login flow does NOT accept the franchisee tester (separate seed/portal-account issue).
context_for_next_testing_agent: If asked to verify /portal/files UI for the franchisee, first confirm whether franchisee.tester@creativemojo.co.uk has a corresponding 'portal account' row (the /portal/login endpoint pre-checks this and 404s before even attempting password verification). The /api/files/search backend hardening is fully covered by /app/backend/tests/test_iter38_files_search.py.
rca_of_the_issue: Original Cloudflare 520 root cause: /api/files/search raised an unhandled exception (most likely _franchisee_scope_filter raising HTTPException(403) for orphan franchisee accounts, or a folder-walk timeout). FastAPI then half-flushed an error response that Cloudflare couldn't parse, surfacing as a 520. Fix wraps the handler in try/except (re-raising HTTPException so 422 validation still works), catches everything else, logs the traceback, and returns 200 with {items:[], files:[], folders:[], count:0, error:'…'}. Bounded folder walk to 20k iterations. Tests confirm the fix holds for the exact prod query and a battery of adversarial inputs.
